Test file: src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Activate test: "should keep a block pattern unchanged when each cell has exactly three neighbors (survival)" (line 14)
Current state: 3 tests passing, 2 todos
Implementation file: src/game-of-life.ts

The test should verify: nextGeneration([[0, 0], [1, 0], [0, 1], [1, 1]]) returns the same set of cells [[0, 0], [1, 0], [0, 1], [1, 1]] — a 2x2 block where each cell has exactly 3 neighbors, so all survive.

Important: Use expect(...).toEqual(expect.arrayContaining(...)) combined with length check, or sort both arrays before comparing, since the order of cells in the output may differ from the input. A simple approach: sort both arrays and use toEqual.

Current implementation is `return []` so this test MUST fail — the expected output is non-empty.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Green after completion.
You MUST output the full Step 7 block verbatim (both Compilation
Prediction and Runtime Prediction lines, each with Correct or
Incorrect). Do not abbreviate. Do not collapse the two prediction
lines into one.

So I skip the compilation error stage -- there is no missing function or type mismatch here.
